entry.title: New Implementation for TH_WHT_50BIS(DRC) Report
entry.content:
Withholding Tax: 

Withholding Tax is a tax that is deducted at the source of income before the recipient receives the payment. It is a legal requirement imposed by governments to ensure that taxes are collected efficiently and timely. The payer of the income withholds or deducts the tax amount and deposits it with the government on behalf of the payee. 

We develop functionalities in SAP ERP systems (like SAP S/4HANA) to manage withholding tax effectively. These functionalities include: 

  • Classic Withholding Tax: Used for simpler tax scenarios where tax is deducted directly during invoice processing. 
  • Extended Withholding Tax: Provides advanced capabilities such as multiple withholding tax types, exemption handling, and reporting requirements. 

Localization and Compliance 

We ensure the withholding tax functionality adheres to specific country legal and compliance requirements: 

  • Country-Specific Solutions: Support withholding tax laws in multiple jurisdictions, incorporating local tax rules and thresholds. 
  • Updates for Legal Changes: Continuously update systems to reflect changes in tax laws, ensuring compliance. 

Reporting and Documentation 

We develop tools and templates for reporting withholding tax: 

  • Tax Certificates: Generate certificates for tax deducted, which the payer provides to the payee. 
  • Statutory Reporting: Support local reporting formats (e.g., Form 26Q in India). 
  • Audit and Reconciliation: Provide detailed records to help businesses reconcile tax payments with statutory filings. 

DRC / ACR Reports: 

Digital Reporting Compliance (DRC) is an SAP cloud-based solution designed to help businesses meet statutory reporting requirements by digitally submitting reports to tax authorities. It is part of SAP’s strategy to address real-time compliance needs, driven by governments globally mandating electronic submissions for various tax-related transactions. 

Basically the implementation is on SEGW Project(ODATA). So , we would change the method _prepare_entity inside the project ‘FDP_FIWTTH_50BIS’ 

We should go DPC_EXT class now 

Go inside _prepare_entity method 

We should add the code inside that method. 

 

WHEN '3500' OR '3600' OR '3700' OR '3800' OR '3900' OR '35A'. 

            IF is_entityset-amountsect405 IS INITIAL AND 
               is_entityset-datesection405 IS INITIAL AND 
               is_entityset-taxsect405 IS INITIAL. 
                      is_entityset-amountsect405 = <ls_consumq>-calcdtxbaseamtincocodecrcy. 
                      is_entityset-datesection405 = <ls_consumq>-postingdate. 
                      is_entityset-taxsect405 = <ls_consumq>-calculatedtxamtincocodecrcy. 
                      is_entityset-withholdingtaxcodename51 = <ls_consumq>-whldgtaxcodename. 
            ELSEIF is_entityset-amountsect4052 IS INITIAL AND 
                   is_entityset-datesection4052 IS INITIAL AND 
                   is_entityset-taxsect4052 IS INITIAL. 
                      is_entityset-amountsect4052 = <ls_consumq>-calcdtxbaseamtincocodecrcy. 
                      is_entityset-datesection4052 = <ls_consumq>-postingdate. 
                      is_entityset-taxsect4052 = <ls_consumq>-calculatedtxamtincocodecrcy. 
                      is_entityset-withholdingtaxcodename52 = <ls_consumq>-whldgtaxcodename. 
            ELSEIF is_entityset-amountsect4053 IS INITIAL AND 
                   is_entityset-datesection4053 IS INITIAL AND 
                   is_entityset-taxsect4053 IS INITIAL. 
                      is_entityset-amountsect4053 = <ls_consumq>-calcdtxbaseamtincocodecrcy. 
                      is_entityset-datesection4053 = <ls_consumq>-postingdate. 
                      is_entityset-taxsect4053 = <ls_consumq>-calculatedtxamtincocodecrcy. 
                      is_entityset-withholdingtaxcodename53 = <ls_consumq>-whldgtaxcodename. 
            ELSE. "If more than 3 income type related to ths (item5) then later will be ignored as max 3 is allowed. 
               lv_linefive_count = 1. 
            ENDIF.

 

The above code we should add instead of below code. 

 

WHEN '3500' OR '3600' OR '3700' OR '3800' OR '3900' OR '35A'. 

     IF lv_linefive_count IS INITIAL. 

              is_entityset-totalwithholdingtax = is_entityset-totalwithholdingtax + <ls_consumq>-calculatedtxamtincocodecrcy. 
              is_entityset-totalwithholdingbase = is_entityset-totalwithholdingbase + <ls_consumq>-calcdtxbaseamtincocodecrcy. 
            
    ENDIF.

 

Here what we have did is, changed the Entity type and written three conditions to store three line items in different lines. For that we should go entity type and add extra 6 fields to store another 6 fields data(Previously, It has only 3 fields to store only one line item, Now, along with that we have added another 6 fields. So that, It can store 3 line items with 3 fields each) 

Those 6 fields we have added. 

Now, If we add extra fields to the entity type, definitely we should change our form(Adobe form) as well 

Now we should go to that form and map the fields to extra fields. 

The form name would be ‘FIWTTH_50BIS’ 

 

Go inside the form.

Go to layout 

Here, we should map the below fields to which we have added the fields in Entity type. 

For output, We should post one Invoice and Payment for the Tax type and code which would have income type as '3500' OR '3600' OR '3700' OR '3800' OR '3900' OR '35A'. 

Before that we will see the Tax codes and types in SPRO configuration. 

After Mapping done, we should go to the Frond end app and should run our report through AP_MANAGER_TH user.

Now, search for Run statutory reports 

Give report name as TH_WHT_50BIS and Reporting Entity name would be ‘TH_WHT_ENT’ 

Click on Go 

Select that report 

Now, we will get our desired out put If we run this report and see the PDF file. 

Previously It was different form that would give us only one line item. 

But now, It would show 3 line items for the 5th line i.e., If the income type is '3500' OR '3600' OR '3700' OR '3800' OR '3900' OR '35A'.

entry.title: Re: Clear some fields when copying a PR to Create a new PR
entry.content:
This Enhancement is provided mostly for customer fields, so should not be very useful.

Better use BAdi ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUS, in an event such as PROCESS_ITEM you can read current data, previous data of new PR and current data of referenced PR, so you should be able to update some fields even standard ones.

  • Look at methods GET_DATA, GET_PREVIOUS_DATA and SET_DATA on received parameters IM_ITEM and IM_REF_ITEM
  • Just insure not to clear twice the field (if user input manually same value) you could set some attributes of the implementing class, reset it in a method such as OPEN or CLOSE.

entry.title: Technical Guide to SAP DMO (Database Migration Option)
entry.content:
Technical Guide to SAP DMO (Database Migration Option)

The SAP DMO (Database Migration Option) toolset simplifies and dramatically streamlines the entire SAP migration with one process, one tool, one downtime. DMO allows SAP users to update an existing SAP system to a higher release version and/or can migrate to SAP HANA database. This blog post is a technical overview on all you need to know about DMO. 

Benefits of DMO 
DMO provides many benefits over other upgrade/migration options by combining ease of use, shortened number of steps, and additional tools for reducing downtime. Here’s a quick overview of the many benefits of DMO. 

  • Migration steps are simplified 
    • Before DMO (Database Migration Option), you needed to update the SAP software with SUM (Software Update Manager) and then migrate it to a new database with SWPM (Software Provisioning Manager). 
    • DMO simplifies the migration with one tool (SUM with DMO) that will run the system update, Unicode conversion, and database migration combined into one tool 
      • If your systems weren’t already Unicode, a Unicode conversion can be included (for single-code-page systems). 
    • A combined procedure only needs one maintenance phase (not two). 
      • Reduces business downtime (TCO) 
      • Less regression test necessary 
    • In place migration keeps the application server and System-ID stable. 
      • Low impact on system landscape, as only the database server is new 
  • Original database is kept and can be reactivated as a fallback 
    • Reduces risk 
    • No restore required 
    • More time for testing before cutover
  • The tool is easy to work with, as it’s just SUM with an improved UI (User Interface) 
  • As of SUM 1.0 SP 13, the benchmarking tool has been added for use as a technique to tune the migration and reduce the technical downtime. 
  • A certification is recommended but is not required for consultants using DMO. 
  • No migration check service is required for DMO on productive systems.

Before you get started 
Before beginning a DMO project, there are a few critical items you should know. With any SAP project, knowing how to navigate the tool and proper planning are the key to success. Here’s a quick list of rules around DMO of SUM. 

  • DMO supports a migration to SAP HANA DB, as well as SAP ASE, MS SQL Server, and IBM DB2. 
    • A homogenous migration is not supported, for example HANA to HANA 
  • DMO can only be started for a maintenance 
    • You need to apply at least on SP, for example ST-PI 
  •  SAP HANA requires a single stack. 
    • DMO only works for AS ABAP based systems: no dual-stack spit option in DMO. 
    • DMO keeps the application server and SID. No switch is possible.
  • For a target system based on SAP Netweaver 7.5 (such as SAP ECC 6.0 EHP8), the source system must be on Unicode already. For this scenario, DMO is not able to cover the Unicode Conversion. 
  • Using DMO for a migration across data center is not supported. There are no technical restrictions, but it comes with a high performance and latency impact. You my use it at your own risk. No support is provided in case of performance issues or broken procedures due to network/latency issues 

                                                                                                                           Published By

                                                                                                                           Nikhat Fatima

entry.title: Authorization Check in S/4HANA Cloud System: Using the Display Authorization Trace App (SU53)
entry.content:
Login to the respective system Tenant (DEV,QAS,PRD) .Search the App Display Authorization Trace. In the App You have to activate Trace for the particular User for the certain time period.

In SU53 screen session where we can get the authorization error of the particular User for that day. No need to recreate the process which creates authorization restriction error.

But in the Display Authorization Trace Cloud App we have to activate the Trace for the particular USER and process recreation is needed for identifying the error.

 

1. Activate Trace : Click Activate Trace Button on the top right corner.
2. Select the USER ID which facing the Authorization Restriction Error, and click ok to enable the trace.
3. Inform the USER to recreate the error .
4.Once recreated Click Deactivate Trace and check the Logs.

In the Logs Authorization Filter Status is Successful / Failed / Filtered

Further analysis of Failed and Filtered Logs of the USER allows us to find the Role Restriction(Restricted / Unrestricted) or missing Field inputs. Fixing the error resolves the Authorization Restriction error.

By following these steps, you can pinpoint and resolve authorization issues efficiently

All The Best.

Cloud SAP Cloud for Customer integration with SAP S/4HANA SAP HANA service for SAP BTP

entry.title: How SAP BTP Enhances Fiori Applications for Modern Workflows
entry.content:
Integrating SAP BTP Services into Fiori Applications

In this short article we’re looking at some core services provided by SAP BTP that can make our Fiori apps way better. BTP has tools that help us do things faster and make work easier. These are some of the services which my team have worked quite a lot lately and the examples shared below are just a high level explanation

With BTP, we can automate tasks (like creation of sales orders), make apps work well on mobile devices, set up notifications from other systems, and even add AI to help with predictions and smart decisions. All these services together make Fiori apps more flexible and powerful, so they actually fit what users need and save everyone a lot of hassle.

 

 

Core BTP Services for Fiori

SAP Build Process Automation (Workflow + IRPA)

SAP BTP’s Workflow service automates and manages business processes seamlessly within Fiori applications. Workflow allows for the creation of multi-step approval chains, task assignments, and process tracking. For example, an organization can implement a purchase order approval workflow within a Fiori app, where each approval or rejection step is automatically updated in real time. This eliminates manual processing and reduces turnaround time, enhancing operational efficiency.

IRPA complements workflows by automating repetitive, manual tasks like data entry or validations. For instance, an IRPA bot can process incoming invoices, validate them against purchase orders, and flag discrepancies for approval in the Fiori app.

Process Visibility offers end-to-end insights into workflows, providing transparency and analytics. Stakeholders can monitor live status, identify bottlenecks, and ensure SLAs are met, enabling proactive process management.

Business Rules provide flexibility in defining approval criteria. For example, an organization can configure dynamic rules based on factors like transaction value or department, ensuring that high-value approvals escalate automatically to senior management. This customization ensures streamlined and compliant operations.

Practical Scenario for SBPA: Using SAP SBPA for automating tenant onboarding in a real estate app.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: Tenant applies to move into a property.
  • Step 2: Workflow triggers and sends an approval request to the property manager.
  • Step 3: Property manager reviews and clicks “Approve” or “Reject” in the app.
  • Step 4: If approved, a notification goes to finance for deposit processing.
  • Step 5: Finance team processes payment and updates status.
  • Step 6: Maintenance team gets a notification to prep the apartment (cleaning, repairs, etc.).
  • Step 7: Once everything’s ready, tenant gets a welcome message with move-in date.

Benefits:

  • Saves time with automated approvals.
  • Clear process, everyone knows what’s next.
  • Reduces delays; no need to email or follow up manually.
           

Mobile Services

SAP Mobile Platform for BTP enable Fiori applications to be fully accessible on mobile devices, with features supporting multi-device compatibility and offline functionality. Users can interact with applications even without an active internet connection, with updates automatically synchronized once connectivity is restored. For example, in field operations, technicians can update service records on a Fiori app even in remote areas, ensuring that data is captured in real time and reflected across the organization when they’re back online.

 

Practical Scenario for Mobile Services: Maintenance team using a Fiori app to log repairs on-site.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: Worker opens the Fiori app on their mobile device while at the repair site.
  • Step 2: They fill out details (e.g., parts replaced, issue resolved) in the app, even without internet.
  • Step 3: App saves the data offline.
  • Step 4: Later, once back online, the app syncs the data with central records.

Benefits:

  • Workers can log repairs immediately, no waiting for internet.
  • Data syncs when there’s a connection, so no missed updates.
  • Reduces paperwork, everything’s in the app.

 

Advanced Event Mesh

SAP Event Mesh enables asynchronous communication, ensuring that Fiori applications can respond to events from various backend systems without delay. This service facilitates real-time event-driven responses, useful in scenarios like order processing or inventory updates. For example, when stock levels reach a predefined threshold, Event Mesh triggers a notification in the Fiori app, allowing users to act immediately to restock, thus preventing delay.

Practical Scenario for Event Mesh: Using Event Mesh in a Fiori app for tracking supply chain inventory.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: Warehouse hits low inventory level for an important product.
  • Step 2: Event Mesh detects this and triggers an alert.
  • Step 3: Fiori app instantly shows a notification for the purchasing team.
  • Step 4: Purchasing team checks the notification and orders more stock.

Benefits:

  • Teams can act immediately to prevent shortages.
  • No need to check inventory manually; they get alerts.
  • Keeps operations smooth by handling restocking on time.

SAP AI Core and AI Foundation

SAP AI Core provide machine learning and AI capabilities that integrate directly into Fiori applications. These services support predictive analytics and automated decision-making, helping users make data-driven decisions efficiently. A practical use case is in a sales application, where predictive analytics might forecast inventory requirements based on historical data, allowing users to prepare and manage resources proactively

 

Practical Scenario for SAP AI Core: Sales team using Fiori app with AI to manage inventory.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: AI model in Fiori app analyses past sales data and predicts future demand.
  • Step 2: Based on prediction, the app suggests optimal stock levels.
  • Step 3: Sales team sees recommended order amounts right in the app.
  • Step 4: They order the suggested amount to avoid running out or over-ordering.

Benefits:

  • Helps sales team avoid stock issues by planning ahead.
  • Reduces wasted stock, as predictions are data-driven.
  • Makes ordering decisions faster and more accurate.

Conclusion

Using BTP services really levels up our Fiori apps. We can set up automated workflows, support mobile use, get instant alerts when things change, and bring in AI to plan smarter. These features make the apps more helpful and let them handle new demands.

It makes the app experience smoother for everyone using it and helps the business run better too. With BTP, our Fiori apps aren’t just tools—they become something people can rely on every day to get things done more easily.

entry.title: How to create an on-demand filter in SAC with linked Analysis
entry.content:
Hi SAP community!

I'd like to present a solution for a scenario which was necessary in my project. Although such scenarios are uncommon, we, as developers, must adapt to meet customer requirements and ensure successful outcomes.

Problem Statement:

Consider this simple scenario where we need to create an input control that can selectively filter the charts on a page. At the same time, we want to preview the overall value in one chart without getting filtered by the input control. Typically, this can be done by setting up linked analysis, which links the filter to the necessary charts and leaves out the charts that don’t need filtering.

However, sometimes the customer might also want an on-demand functionality where they want to filter the other KPI whenever needed. This functionality cannot be directly provided by the inputcontrol's linked analysis. The filtering can either be set as enabled or disabled for that particular chart. To handle this unique scenario, we need to implement a small workaround.

Solution:

In order to handle the requirement, I have created a simple workaround process for creating the on-demand filtering functionality. All location selected

 

Selected Location OnlyBased on the screenshot attached above, 

When we choose 'Selected Location' in the list box, the filtering will only occur to the numeric point based on the selected location. If it is set to 'All Locations' It will display the overall value.

Prerequisites:

In order to achieve this, we need

  • two input controls,
  • a list box,
  • a variable and
  • a few charts.

Objects Outline

Step by step approach:

1.Creating Input controls and setting up Linked Analysis

Create two input controls with the same dimension 'Location'

Our first input control, (LocationFilter) should be linked to only to the bar chart. (Chart_Gross_Margin)

Our second input control, (HiddenFilter) can be kept hidden and it should be linked only to the Numeric point chart. (NP_Gross_Margin)

The purpose of the first input control is to pass the selected value onto the second input control only on Demand.

Since the 2nd input control is the only control linked to the numeric point, any values passed to 2nd input control will automatically filter the Numeric point and show the filtered values.

2.Create variable and a List box

We need one variable for capturing the changes made in the input control and a list box for providing the option to choose between all members and filtered members.

3. Scripting to pass the variable value from Inputcontrol 1 to Inputcontrol 2

Now that we have all the necessary objects and variables created. The script has to be created in the respective objects.

The below script will get the value selected from the first input control and will pass it to the second input control if the 'ID1' (Selected Location) option in the listbox is selected.

Scripting done at Input control

 

//For getting the selected value from the Location InputControl
var_fieldcapture = LocationFilter.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers()[0].id;

//If the listbox member 'Selected Location' is specified, the variable should pass to the 2nd input control
if(ListBox.getSelectedKey()==='ID1')
	{
	HiddenFilter.getInputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembers(var_fieldcapture); //Passes variable value to hidden input control
	}

 

Scripting done at Listbox

 

//All Locations
if(ListBox.getSelectedKey()==='All')
	{
	HiddenFilter.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected(); //selects all members in the hidden input control
	}

//Selected Locations
if(ListBox.getSelectedKey()==='ID1')
	{
	HiddenFilter.getInputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembers(var_fieldcapture); //Passes the variable value to the hidden input control
	}

 

Note: This logic will work for single selection of the Input control, for multiple selection Array iteration has to be performed and has to be passed from Input control 1 to Input control 2.

Conclusion:
I hope this blog offers a clear perspective on creating an OnDemand filter with Linked Analysis. By adopting such methodologies, you might find ways to enhance your development process and potentially save time.

Hope this article was helpful.🙂

entry.title: Re: key figure calculation past-current-future logic and...
entry.content:
Hi @Daniele_Cuozzo,

this can be achieved by adding an IF statement to your calculated KF3 in the following way:

IF(("PERIODID3" => "$$PERIODID3CU$$"), "KF2", "KF1")

The number within the PERIODID represents the time level of your time profile. In this case 3 stands for months. However you need to reveal the correct number based on your indiviually used time profile.

Your other questions are related to the same logic and can be solved with small additions.

BR, Peter

entry.title: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
In engineering, every solution involves trade-offs—it's about weighing the pros and cons. The same applies to HN, which isn’t all evil. Back in the days, when we just gave up on GO TO instruction (hail to Edsger Dijkstra) and had only 72 characters for one line of code, marking variables as local/global was a way to manage situation, where we use 20 locals and 30 globals within one procedure and it's hard to guess what is what. Limited length of names didn't help either, so all those "ls_" had some significant advantages.

But times have changed. We now have modern IDEs like Eclipse and ABAP OO, allowing us to write smaller, more manageable routines. Most of the limitations that existed in the early days of ABAP are no longer an issue. As a result, the benefits of HN have disappeared, leaving only the drawbacks, which actively harm the quality of code.

Harm #1: It Encourages Laziness and poor Naming

Real life example (just one of many): gt_vbak, gt_vbak2, lt_vbak1, lt_vbak2. These names I had encountered in a > 10k  long report. Trying to make sense of this was maddening. Eventually I decided to rename despite having no access to Eclipse on that system. The new names were:

  • gt_vbak = orders
  • gt_vbak2 = quotations
  • lt_vbak1 = orders_wo_quotation (in one routine, all orders not originating from quotations were extracted and processed)
  • lt_vbak2 = rejected orders -yes sir! Digit 2 in the name didn't indicate quotations, they were also orders 🙂 .

The irony is that these tables weren't even based on the vbak type but were locally defined. So keeping 'vbak' in the name.

I know, nothing stops us from making meaningful names with HN, so gt_partners and ls_partner are possible. And yet almost no one writes gt_partners. What's worse, some create gt_partner and ls_partner. What is gt_partner in this case? It sounds like a table of data for one partner only, but I guess it's not.

To summarise: You could make good names with HN, but practice shows, that no one does. Whereas banning prefixes forces us to spend a minute on creating a proper name. It's not a technical issue. In fact it's a matter of human  nature.

Harm #2: It Makes Code Harder to Read

In the example above, no matter how familiar you are with prefixes (I had 10 years of prefixing ingrained in me), you’ll still mix up lt_vbak2 with gt_vbak2 every few minutes. It’s just impossible not to. These aren’t real words, so we don’t process them efficiently.

Our brains are wired to recognize words based on patterns. If the frist and last letters of a word are correct and the middle letters are slightly jumbled, we can still read it as if it were correct, sometimes without even notciing the errors 😉. This shows that our brains help us read familiar, pronounceable words , but offer no such support for prefixes like lt_, gs_, and so on.

A friend of mine once decided to try coding without prefixes. For the first two weeks, he cursed me and my descendants to the 20th generation, as breaking a habit of 10 years was tough. But after three weeks, he said it felt like seeing clearly for the first time. Code without lt_ reads like a novel - like human language - whereas prefixed code felt like hieroglyphics. Sure, if you’re used to hieroglyphics, you can read them quickly, but it’s still much slower than reading a regular novel.

As Uncle Bob once said, adding prefixes is like adding unnecessary code to the code itself. When reading, my brain first has to decode the prefixes, and only then can it process the ABAP. Writing in plain, human-readable language lets us focus entirely on the ABAP itself.

Harm #3: It Impairs Our Way of Thinking

When using HN, it influences how we use code completion.: I write a code and now I need to get something from table of orders. So I don't bother thinking what was its name - it's enough to type "lt_" and hit ctrl+space.

When I gave up on HN, I had to start using meaningful names (because there was no option to add prefix to the terrible, 4-letter table name). Now, I "search" for variables by their name, not their type. I know I’m looking for a table of customers, so I type "cus" and hit ctrl+Space. After three months of working like this, I found myself improving the clarity of my variable names even further. I even started creating "families" of names, such as orders_rejected, orders_closed, and orders_in_processing, as it helps utilise code completion even further. At the same time code becomes even clearer.

Sticking with prefixes would have never allow me to improve this way.

Harm #4: It Misleads, It Lies

Thousands of times have I seen:

  • lt_kna1, which was actually global.
  • lv_something which was a structure, not a variable,
  • ls_bkpf which was not of a bkpf structure,
  • and many other lies.

This led to a habit where, if scope (l/g) really mattered, I would always check (by double click in SE80 or glimpse on the overview ruler in Eclipse). So I ignore letter l/g - it became a noise for me. The same goes for types - I never trust the letter and I check the type by myself (F2 in Eclipse). This double-check actually saves some time (and nerves).

Honestly, I’d rather have no information at all than false information (the same criticism applies to misleading comments, but that’s another story).

Harm #5: It Increases the Entry Threshold

There is some general agreement in ABAP world, on which prefixes to use...

Oh is there?!? wa_, or ls_? What about mv_ for the "member" attribute in class? What if it's a static? By definition It's not a member, so should we still call it mv_ or maybe sv_?

How about field symbols? When I see <fs_some_data> it makes me fill like this:

Pulp Fiction - Any Of You Pricks Move

I mean come on, didn't you see the angle brackets <>? Are they not enough?

But what about <ls_some_data>? Is it a field symbol, or a structure? What if we agreed on prefixing field symbols, but this one is of any type?

And what about objects? After all, they are all references. So some will call them lr_sth, and some others lo_sth, whereas "lr_" will be reserved only for non-object references (but here you loose information if it's a reference to a variable, structure or a table!)

STOP! Go back to "wa_". Oh my, I don't know if it's local or global. The same with <fs_...>.  I'm so confused 🤕!

Well, you end up with some naming rules and each new member of the team has to learn them and get used to them even though they bring no value. What's weird - prefixes are more required from the freshman than proper naming in general.

Harm #6: It Creates Inconsistent Styles

Because of the reasons above, one person in a program might use wa_ (making it unclear if the variable is local or global), while another uses ls_ or gs_. Some might use it_ (which I think stands for internal table), while others use lt_ or gt_. Then there’s the lr_ vs. lo_ debate. In the end, what we have is not a program, but a riddle.

Nothing forces us to make this mess... except human nature. I’ve seen plenty of programs suffering from MNA (Mixed Naming Arts. 😉  ).

 

Since I have mentioned mixed naming styles I'd like to point out, that it's absolutely essential to follow the existing style (or to rename the whole code). Having two naming styles is always worse than having one, no matter how bad the style is.

What Stops Us From Ditching HN?

Basically, the only thing that holds us from dropping HN is habit. After using it for years, we think it’s easier because it’s all we know.

It takes about 3-4 weeks to retrain your brain, and I guarantee you won’t lose the ability to read old, prefixed code. You’ll still be able to read and write it.

However, giving up HN won’t noticably improve your code unless it’s part of a broader set of changes, including:

  • Meaningful naming (without HN)
  • Small routines (methods)
  • Single responsibility

This short list is just the beginning, but following these three principles alone (along with giving up on prefixes) will significantly improve the quality and maintainability of your code.

What Now, What To Follow?

If not HN, then which style should I follow? If you want some clues, please refer to CleanABAP. It's published as a book and also available on GitHub:

https://github.com/SAP/styleguides/blob/main/clean-abap/CleanABAP.md

 

Since I have started the topic, I guess I will continue and elaborate on this short list in next blog posts.

entry.title: End-to-End Testing with SAP Cloud ALM - Overview
entry.content:
Introduction:

End-to-end testing with SAP Cloud ALM (Application Lifecycle Management) focuses on the execution of end-to-end tests within SAP landscapes, integrating both SAP solutions and third-party systems using the tools and services provided by SAP Cloud ALM for testing. This platform offers a modern and comprehensive solution for design, implementation, test, deploy and traceability of business processes. In this blog post, I present an overview of the functions available in SAP Cloud ALM for end-to-end testing and their benefits.

Before I start describing the functionalities, I would like to define SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation and E2E testing.

What is SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation?

SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation is a cloud-based Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) solution designed to streamline and manage the implementation of SAP cloud solutions. It offers a comprehensive suite of tools and methodologies to support the entire implementation lifecycle, ensuring efficient project execution and alignment with business objectives. It supports the ‘Requirement to-Deploy’ process which SAP Cloud ALM follows.

 

For end-to-end testing (E2E), the SAP Cloud ALM ensures integration with SAP systems and processes, including interfaces to non-SAP systems.

What is End-to-end testing (E2E)?

End-to-End (E2E) Integration Testing according to ISTQB is a testing methodology aimed at validating the complete flow of a system, ensuring that all integrated components or systems work together as expected. This type of testing focuses on the full business process and goes beyond individual interfaces or module-level integration. This is a test procedure in which complete business processes are tested from start to finish to ensure that all systems and components work together smoothly. The aim is to ensure that the application works as expected and that changes do not cause unexpected errors.

 

Features of end-to-end testing (E2E) with SAP Cloud ALM:

SAP Cloud ALM provides comprehensive end-to-end testing functionalities, which will also be continuously enhanced. Currently, not all functions required for seamless E2E testing are available. It is currently manageable through workarounds, but each new feature and the planned enhancements will continue to improve the process significantly. According to the SAP roadmap, the following current and planned features support end-to-end testing:

1. Test Planning and Orchestration

  • Test Planning: Plan and structure test execution and enable organization and management of test cases across different test cycles. (Recommendation: Use a proper naming convention for the test plans as well as for test cases (e.g. an ID for a project) to be able to find and filter/sort them in a useful way for E2E testing)
  • Tester assignment and report for test plans: Assigning of testers to test cases and monitoring of test execution based on test plans

  • Support of test sequences in test plans: A planned feature to create test sequences within test plans for the execution of test cases (for end-to-end scenarios). (link to  Roadmap - Support of test sequences in test plans)

Benefits:

  • Simplifies planning and execution of complex testing scenarios
  • Enables structured testing for better test coverage

 2. Testing

  • Test execution: Execution and documentation of test results with the option to add comments and screenshots as well as for Defect Management in test execution

 

  • Tags: Tagging of test cases allows users to assign tags to test cases for better organization and categorization as well as enabling to filter and search based on tags to easily locate relevant test cases

 

  • Relating of test cases to the process hierarchy: Assign your test cases to the process hierarchy nodes
  • Reference links for test cases: Adding reference links to test cases (e.g. to provide test data sheets)
  • Integration of test cases in processes: A planned feature for the creation of test cases within processes and linking existing test cases to processes (link to Roadmap - Integration of test cases in processes)
  • Document-based test cases: A planned feature for directly creating and managing test cases based on documents. (link to Roadmap - Document-based test cases)
  • Test evidence: A planned feature to mark a test action as ‘Evidence required’ to guide testers during test execution (link to Roadmap - Test evidence)

Benefits:

  • Ensures traceability and alignment with business processes
  • Facilitates detailed documentation for auditing and compliance
  • Improves visibility into the entire testing lifecycle

3. Test Automation

  • Integration with Automation Tools and TTA (Tricentis Test Automation): Support for cloud-based test automation tools like Tricentis for SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • Test Variants: Management of test variants in both preparation and execution phases.

Benefits:

  • Reduces manual effort and speeds up in testing outcomes

4. Traceability and Analytics

  • Traceability: Linking requirements, user stories and test cases to ensure comprehensive test coverage.
  • Analytics: Providing reports and insights into test execution and defect tracking.

 

 

Benefits:

  • Ensures full test coverage and compliance
  • Improves visibility int the entire testing cycle
  • Facilitates data-driven decisions through detailed reporting

 

These features aim to make the testing process for E2E-testing in SAP Cloud ALM more efficient and transparent by supporting both manual and automated methods and seamlessly integrating them into the application lifecycle.

Some other interesting functionalities planned for testing include Mass upload of test cases, Mass editing of test cases and Test plan copy. Besides this, we also have one function marked as product visions for the future: API for test cases for uploading of partner and customer-owned test cases. Further functionalities planned or marked as a product vision are listed in the Road Map Explorer with the upcoming releases.

Conclusion:

End-to-end testing with SAP ALM Cloud is an efficient way to ensure the quality and stability of business processes. With integrated features for process documentation, test management, automation as well as reporting, SAP ALM Cloud helps companies to manage their SAP landscapes efficiently and implement changes securely. With the planned functions, SAP Cloud ALM offers even more benefits and exciting features for E2E testing in the future. Stay curious!

Thank you for reading and I am happy to receive your feedback, questions, or suggestions!

entry.title: Field Control for Developer Extensibility Custom Fields
entry.content:
Field Control for Developer Extensibility Custom Fields

Do you want to influence the field properties of your developer extensibility custom fields in SAP standard UIs? Do you want to configure them as mandatory or read-only or invisible, even dynamically, based on specific business data? 

Please note that there is another blog post that gives an introduction into the topic of field control for custom fields. It also guides you how to find out which detail blog you should look at in your specific scenario.
The reason we have several specific blogs is that what you must do to implement field control for custom field differs significantly for key user custom fields and developer extensibility custom fields.
When you already know that in your case, it’s about developer extensibility custom fields, then this blog is just for you.

This blog post covers the following topics for developer extensibility custom fields:

  • How you can find out whether the respective application supports developer extensibility custom fields
  • whether field control is supported in this case,
  • and if so, how to set your custom fields mandatory or read-only in a static or dynamic way

Please note that this blog doesn't only address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but also SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.

Developer extensibility allows you to add custom fields to Fiori applications which are based on the RAP programming model. You can find detailed information in the documentation for the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/develop-data-model-extensions.

Field control for developer extensibility custom fields is natively built into the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. As field control is part of the behavior of a RAP business object, it needs to be added to the Behavior Definition (BDEF). Therefore, the BDEF needs to be enabled and released for Extend (C0 Contract) by SAP Standard Development.

See also https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/develop-behavior-extensions.

If these prerequisites are met, you can define access restrictions for fields in a static way so that these restrictions are valid for each instance. The following field properties for static field control are possible:

  • Read-Only (always or only via update)
  • Mandatory

If the field control should be specific for a certain Fiori application or OData service, this can be done with an extension on projection level. This is possible for the field characteristics readonly and readonly:update.

All these characteristics are also possible if the behavior of the field should be related to the business object and be active for all projections on this business object. In this case, the base behavior definition needs to be extended. The mandatory behavior is also available here. It can’t be defined specifically per projection.

Using developer extensibility, it’s also possible to define and implement dynamic field control based on certain conditions. You can define a field as

  • Read-only
  • Mandatory
  • Without restrictions

Dynamic field control needs to be added to the base behavior definition. The reason is that it requires an implementation which can only be added on the base layer in BDEF extensions using a released RAP BO interface.

If you want to learn more about feature control in RAP, have look here.

Example – Fiori App “Manage Sales Orders – Version 2” (Fiori App ID F3893)

Let’s start with an example to explain all options and necessary steps.

We have extended the RAP business object “SalesOrder” on header and item level with custom fields using the extension field wizard. The fields have also been added to the CDS projection views which are used in the Fiori app Manage Sales Orders – Version 2 (Fiori App ID F3893).

It’s important that we also add the custom fields to the data model of the released RAP BO interface for the SalesOrder, because we want to use the fields in our implementation for the field control of the RAP business object.

This means that the fields need to be added to the CDS views I_SalesOrderTP (Header) or I_SalesOrderItemTP (Item) respectively.

Next, we want to define the following field control for these fields:

Entity

Field

Field Control

Header

ZZDiscountPercentageSDH

Static Read-Only on Projection

ZZPostponeDeliverySDH

-

ZZDeliveryUpdateMailAddressSDH

Dynamic

Item

ZZClassificationSDI

Static Mandatory

ZZPrefQuantityPerDeliverySDI

Dynamic

  1. Static Read-Only on Projection

To define a field as read-only for a certain projection, we need to create a behavior definition extension for this projection. Right-click on the behavior definition in the Project Explorer view in ABAP development tools for Eclipse and choose to create a new behavior definition extension. Set the correct package and name for the extension and click Finish.

Define the respective custom field as read-only in the behavior extension of the extended entity.

The BDEF syntax is field (readonly).

If you want to set the field only to read-only during update operations, you could also use the syntax field (readonly:update)

  1. Static Mandatory

The field characteristic “Mandatory“ can only be defined on the level of the business object. Hence, a BDEF extension for the business object R_SalesOrderTP needs to be created. Here, you also need to specify the released RAP BO interface which should be used to access the released part of the RAP business object in the BDEF extension and its implementation.

Next, define the field as mandatory.

A mandatory field also requires a validation which checks the mandatory field. See below for more information on how to implement a mandatory validation.

  1. Dynamic Field Control

For dynamic field control, we should also extend the behavior of the RAP business object together with the RAP BO interface, as we need to provide an implementation for it, too.

The implementation is done in the respective behavior pool. The method get_instance_features for each relevant entity is generated into this class. Here, we need to add the logic for our field control. On the item, we set the field ZZPrefQuantityPerDeliverySDI to read-only once the overall delivery status is completed, since otherwise, it's editable.

On the header, we set the field ZZDeliveryUpdateMailAddressSDH to mandatory if the delivery status is not yet completed and the custom field ZZPostponeDeliverySDH is set to true. The field is read-only if the delivery status is completed, since otherwise, it's editable.

Mandatory Validation

Now, we have defined fields as mandatory, both static and dynamic. However, the definition of the behavior doesn't imply automatic mandatory validations. You should implement these too to make your custom field entirely mandatory.

How to implement such a validation is specific to the application which is extended. RAP Validation extensibility is not available for RAP-unmanaged applications like the sales order. However, these applications sometimes offer BAdIs which are called during the unmanaged implementation of the respective application. For our sales order example, the relevant BAdI is located in the enhancement spot ES_SD_SLS_EXTEND. It's the BAdI SD_SLS_CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE which is called during the save sequence of the sales order.

The implementation could look like this:

With that, we have defined different field control characteristics for our custom fields using developer extensibility.

The result with multiple custom fields, their field control, and before save validation looks like this:

Now, you are done! Congratulations!
With this you are now equipped to

  • find out whether the Fiori app based on RAP at hand supports field control for custom fields and
  • if so, you know what to do to implement the field control logic.

Feedback, Comments, or Questions:

You’re cordially invited to provide them in the comments section below. Please follow our profiles to get updates when we publish more posts on these topics.
Feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/).

Other related blog posts:

  1. Custom Fields in S/4HANA: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility
  2. Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties (mandatory,...)
  3. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties
  4. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding
  5. Custom fields in S/4HANA: Key User versus Developer Extensibility
  6. SAP Screen Personas: How to position key user custom fields exactly where you want them to be

entry.title: Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding
entry.content:
Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding

Do you want to influence the field properties of your custom fields in Fiori UIs which are based on OData services exposed with service definition and service binding? Do you want to set these fields as mandatory or read-only or invisible, even dynamically, based on specific business data?

Please note that there is another blog post that introduces the topic of field control for custom fields. It also guides you on how to find out which of these detail blogs you should look at in your specific scenario.
The reason why we have several detail blogs is that what you have to do to implement field control for custom fields heavily depends on the standard UIs’ implementation technology.
When you already know that in your scenario, you have a Fiori UI at hand which is based on an OData service exposed with service definition and service binding, then this blog post is just for you.

This blog post covers the following topics:

  • How to check if your Fiori UI supports key user custom fields
  • Whether field control is available for those fields, and if so:
    • How to set your custom fields mandatory or read-only in a static way

Please note that this blog doesn’t only address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but also SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.

We have different means to control the visibility and behavior of custom fields in OData services which are defined with a service definition and service binding.  Where the visibility is controlled by a UI annotation of the exposed CDS entity, the behavior (read-only and mandatory) is defined in the RAP behavior definition if the app is a transactional application.

Currently, not all of the RAP-based SAP standard applications support this feature. In case it turns out that the SAP standard RAP application in your scenario is not custom field-enabled at all, or field control is not supported, please create a respective extensibility enabling request via the customer influence channel.
For the visibility control and the static read-only behavior, you need to find out the relevant CDS projection view. For the static mandatory feature and the developer extensibility support, you need to find out the underlying CDS BO view. You can do so by finding the name of the service binding in the Implementation Information -> Configuration for the respective Fiori app in the Fiori Apps Library. From the service binding in ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) you can directly navigate to the service definition that’s used. Here, you’ll find all the CDS entities which are exposed in this service.

If you have access to SAP GUI (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or On-Premise) you can open the Extensibility CDS context for this CDS view (same name) in ADT. A SAP GUI window shows the key user extensibility enabling of this view. Under the tab Support of Custom Fields, you can check if a BAdI is maintained to support dynamically hiding custom fields, and also whether fields can be set to read-only (displayed as a checkbox in the related business contexts table). If you prefer SAP GUI, you can also start transaction SCFD_REGISTRY and search for the relevant CDS view instead of directly opening it in ADT.

If you don’t have access to SAP GUI, the only way to check if the respective feature is available is in the Custom Fields app itself. Once you have created a custom field for a business context, you can see the supported features in the details of a user interface displayed for this field in the app.

Figure 1: User Interface Details - showing available features

Visibility Control

Let’s start with the visibility of a field for a Fiori UI based on such a service. Technically, the visibility of a field is controlled by the CDS annotation @UI.hidden: true/false. This can be done in a dynamic way if the value is represented by an abap.char(1) field which represents the values ‘X’ for true and ‘’ for false and whose value is determined dynamically. Key users can leverage this by implementing a BAdI in case this is offered by the respective application. The dynamic visibility control does only work for object pages, not for lists.

More details on how the dynamic hiding of fields on an object page works is described in this documentation.

The Custom Fields app supports the definition of dynamically hidden fields for applications which have been enabled for this. If this is the case, you’ll see an option to provide visibility control in the detail settings when enabling a user interface.

Let’s have a look at an example:

In the app for Mass Change of Sales Documents (App ID F5091), you can change the values of multiple sales documents (such as sales orders) at once. A job is scheduled that shows the result of these changes. When displaying the job details with the app Monitor Mass Changes of Sales Documents (App ID F5210), you can also display custom fields. To ensure that only custom fields are displayed which have been changed during the mass change, we can leverage the dynamic visibility control feature.

A custom field has been added to the business context sales documents and enabled for the UI usage Mass Change of Sales Document Header Change as shown in the figure below.

Figure 2: Custom Field "Discount in %"

This field has been mass-changed with the job SD_Mass_Update_Discount_09/08/24. Another job was scheduled (SD_Mass_Update_08/08/24) which did not change this field.

Now, we want to display the field in the job details only in case it has been changed. Otherwise, it should be hidden, just as displayed in the figures below.

Figure 3: Fiori App Monitor Mass Changes of Sales Documents (App ID F5210) – custom field is invisible

Figure 4: Fiori App Monitor Mass Changes of Sales Documents (App ID F5210) – custom field is visible

To be able to achieve this behavior, we need to go back to the Custom Fields app and select the field Discount in %, open the User Interfaces section again and navigate to the details of the selected UI usage Mass Change of Sales Document Header Change for the CDS view C_SLSDOCMASSCHGREQHDRCHANGETP as shown in the figure below.

Figure 5: Custom Fields App - Navigate to Details of UI Enabling

Here, you can either select an existing visibility control implementation, or create a new one. If you choose to create a new implementation, you will be forwarded to the Custom Logic app and you’ll create and implement a custom logic extension point. See the figures below:

Figure 6: Select Visibility Control implementation or create a new one

Figure 7: Define new custom logic implementation

Figure 8: Implement custom logic

After you have implemented the custom logic, you can select it in the Custom Fields app for the respective user interface. Save and publish your changes. Now, you are done, and the app runs with the visibility control logic for your custom field.

Static Mandatory and Read-Only Behavior

Next, we want to learn how to set fields statically to read-only or mandatory. Like visibility control, these features require that the extended application has been prepared for it.

If you want to set a field to read-only, you’d technically need an extension of the behavior definition projection for the relevant service. Within this extension, the field is defined as read-only. As behavior definitions are part of the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), this is only possible for applications which are built with RAP and enabled for extensibility.

Figure 9: Behavior Definition Extension for projection - defining static read-only custom field

Setting a field statically to read-only makes sense if this field is edited through another channel, such as another user interface or a custom logic implementation.

Let’s have a look at the following use case:

A sales order is always linked to a customer (sold-to-party) and each customer can have a customer classification. The classification of a customer can be relevant for further sales order processing and needs to be stored together with other relevant data. The customer classification should not be manually entered on the sales order UI, but rather be determined from the referenced customer and set to read-only on the user interface.

To build this example, we create a custom field Customer Classification for the business context Sales: Sales Documents and enable it for the user interface Manage Sales Orders – Version 2.

Figure 10: Editable Custom Field "Customer Classification"

Then, we create an implementation of the extension point SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD (modification of the header Custom Fields in Sales Documents) with the Custom Logic app. As you can see from the code below, the customer classification is determined from the referenced customer (sold-to-party).

Figure 11: Custom Logic to determine Customer Classification

With that, the field is automatically filled with the correct value, and we can make it read-only on the Manage Sales Orders user interface. To do so, we again go to the Custom Fields app for this field and select the relevant usage.

Figure 12: Custom Field - Navigation to Details of enabled UI

Figure 13: Custom field - Select Read Only Behavior

When we open the sales order application again, we can see that the field is read-only and filled with the determined value.

Figure 14: Manage Sales Orders UI with read-only custom field

Now, we want to set a field mandatory. This does also require a BDEF extension. This is not done in the projection BDEF but in the business object itself. In addition to the mandatory declaration of the custom field, a validation is also required, as mandatory fields are not validated generically.

Figure 15:Behavior Definition Extension - defining static mandatory custom field including on save validation

In the following example, we create a custom field for the business context Master Data: Bill of Material Header (BOM_HEADER) and enable it for the RAP application Manage Order Bill Of Material (Version 2) – Fiori App ID F4339A. This application uses the OData service UI_ORDERBILLOFFMATERIAL.

Figure 16: Custom field "Important Information" enabled for UI service UI_ORDERBILLOFFMATERIAL

In this application, the field should be mandatory and if it’s not filled, it should raise an error message and avoid saving the document without a value. To achieve this, we can navigate into the details of this field usage. Here, we get the option to set the behavior for the custom field in this service.

Figure 17: Set mandatory behavior for the custom field

After we have saved and published the field, we can make it visible in the application using the Adapt UI functionality. The field is then displayed as mandatory and is also validated accordingly.

Figure 18: Mandatory Custom Field - showing error when initial

Now, you are done! Congratulations!
With this, you are now equipped to

  • find out whether the Fiori UI at hand supports field control for custom fields and
  • if so, you know how to code your field control logic.

Feedback, Comments, or Questions:

You’re cordially invited to provide them in the comments section below. Please follow our profiles to get updates when we publish more posts on these topics.
Feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/).

Other related blog posts:

  1. Custom Fields in S/4HANA: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility
  2. Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties (mandatory,...)
  3. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties
  4. Field Control for Developer Extensibility Custom Fields
  5. Custom fields in S/4HANA: Key User versus Developer Extensibility
  6. SAP Screen Personas: How to position key user custom fields exactly where you want them to be

element: 'DateField',

usage: #FILTER_AND_RESULT

 

}

entry.title: Re: Procurement of Services in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
With SAP Ariba 2411 (Nov 2024) Release, we have these 2 new Features for Services:

  1. New User Group for Force Rejecting Service Sheets [ACSS-1634]
    This feature introduces a new user group, Force Reject Service Sheet, that can be assigned to a user who can force-reject service sheets that are in the Processed state in the SAP Ariba solution. You can force-reject a service sheet that the designated approvers previously approved. For more details, refer Force-rejecting a Service Sheet.
  2. Support of Update Operation for Service Entry Sheet [NP-40603]
    This feature supports the 'Update' operation for SES indicating that the SES, when rejected by buyer user in backend ERP is updated and resubmitted. Earlier when SES was resubmitted with the same number, there was no indication that it is updated. Now the SES when resubmitted is not rejected by buyer's backend ERP system when the operation is update.
    Note: The SES with operation as update is only supported for Services Orders with Lean Services / Enhanced limits.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Subscription Billing (October and November 2024)
entry.content:
Hey there, remember us? We're back with more exciting updates and some fantastic new functionalities to share with you! This time, we're pleased to present several new features that were recently released in October and November. Let's dive in and explore how they can benefit your business:

Subscription Billing Analytics

We’re thrilled to announce the introduction of two new analytics applications:
Subscription Analytics and Billing Data Analytics. These applications are designed to provide comprehensive overviews, development trends, and in-depth analytical insights into your subscriptions and billing data.

Subscription Analytics enables you to make informed business decisions by providing a detailed view of key performance indicators (KPIs) and trends in your subscription data. This, in turn, helps identify growth opportunities and areas needing improvement.

Billing Data Analytics allows you to effectively track billing performance and cash flow, ensuring you can optimize financial operations and address billing anomalies promptly.
For more information, see Analytics.

Change of Main Subscription Billing Cycle

We’ve added more flexibility to the billing cycle assignment for the main subscription item. Previously, changes could only be made to the billing cycles of subscription subitems. Now, you can also change the billing cycle of the main subscription item.
For example, if you're offering a service to your customers with a yearly or monthly billing cycle, this new feature allows your customers to switch from yearly to monthly or vice versa. The enhancement offers greater adaptability in managing subscription billing cycles and enables you to better align billing cycles with customer preferences and business needs. This can lead to improved customer satisfaction and more streamlined billing operations.

Additional Billing Cycles

Billing customers just got a whole lot more flexible! We now support many more anniversary billing cycles, offering greater choice in how you manage billing periods.
With more options for billing cycles, you can tailor billing schedules to better fit the unique financial needs of your customers, making your offerings more attractive and competitive. This can result in enhanced customer loyalty and potentially increased revenue from flexible subscription terms.
For more information about billing cycles, see Billing Cycle.

Weekly Waiver Period for Recurring Charges

We've added another exciting feature to give you even greater billing flexibility! The waiver period for recurring charges has always been a part of our solution, allowing you to offer a limited free-of-charge period for recurring charges at the start of the subscription. This feature provides increased go-to-market options, improves customer acquisition and satisfaction by offering promotional waivers, and simplifies your billing processes.
However, with the introduction of weekly billing cycles, you can now define waiver periods in weeks. This additional flexibility in defining waiver periods offers more granular control over promotional offers, helping you better meet your customers' needs.

That’s all on our latest features. By the way, there were many more features released in October and November. The list with all features can be found in the SAP Subscription Billing "What's New" page of the SAP Help. Thank you for reading today’s blog post! ☁️


To make sure you don’t miss the next feature articles, follow the tag SAP Subscription Billing. And in the meantime, you can find out more about our solution in the information section below.
Also: Please like this post if you found it useful, and don’t hesitate to leave us your feedback in the comment section.
In case of questions, feel free to ask them in our Q&A section using the tag SAP Subscription Billing.


Stay tuned and take care!
Christian

More Information About SAP Subscription Billing

  • What’s New
  • SAP Help Portal Page
  • Feature Overview

entry.title: Re: Category Management with SAP Ariba
entry.content:
With SAP Ariba 2411 (Nov 2024) Release, we have these 6 new features for SAP Ariba Category Management:

  1. Enhancements to Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP Ariba Category Management [CAT-8970]
    This feature allows you to generate articles in the Additional Resources section for tools like Category Segmentation, Market Dynamics, and Cost Structure on demand, rather than by default. This enhances performance and reduces the costs associated with producing AI-generated articles.
  2. Ability to Configure Team Member Roles in SAP Ariba Category Management [CAT-9400]
    This feature enables category management administrators to create, edit, delete, activate, and inactivate team member roles in the Manage Configurations app of SAP Ariba Category Management. Category managers can use these configured team member roles to create team members for categories within the Manage Category Profile app.
  3. Ability to Configure Spend Channel Types in SAP Ariba Category Management [CAT-9401]
    This feature enables category management administrators to configure spend channel types in the Manage Configurations app of SAP Ariba Category Management. By configuring spend channel types, category managers can create tailored spend channels for categories in the Manage Category Profile app.
  4. Ability to Configure and Associate Business Units in SAP Ariba Category Management [CAT-9678]
    With this feature, a Category Management Administrator can create and manage a two-level hierarchy for business units. You can also edit, inactivate, or delete these units as needed. You can associate business units with strategy and plan documents and tools in the toolkit.
  5. Ability to Create Category Strategy Details from the Develop Strategy and Plan App [CAT-9695]
    This feature introduces a new tool called Category Strategy Details in the Develop Strategy and Plan app of SAP Ariba Category Management. This tool enables category managers to create and manage multiple category-specific strategy details, associate them with goals and business requirements & seek approval.
  6. Ability to View, Analyze, and Manage Initiatives Across Categories [CAT-10012]
    This feature introduces a new app, Initiatives Overview that allows you to view initiatives data in form of customizable tables and charts, filter and sort data and perform other such operations on data. This app enables you to manage, track, and optimize initiatives effectively.

For more details on these, kindly refer this link.

entry.title: Re: AI in SAP Ariba Invoicing - Intelligent Invoice Conversion
entry.content:
With SAP Ariba 2411 (Nov 2024) Release, we have this new Feature for Intelligent Invoice Conversion:

Identify Buyer Using TaxID and PO number for Invoices Sent Through Email [NP-48723] 
This feature resolves the mandate of entering the ANID of the buyer in the subject line of the email. This feature now processes the invoice attached through mail correctly irrespective of whether the supplier enters the ANID or not. If ANID is not entered in the subject line of the email, the ANID of the buyer is identified from the attachment before conversion.

  •  Feature Preview
  •  5 minutes Demo

entry.title: Inspection point not created - doubt with key date of routing
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a problem with the generation of inspection point in relation to inspection type 03 when client creates manually a new routing, since with routing already created with massive migration there is not the problem.

So a Planner Production create a Routing and then a quality Responsible change the routing (with key date of routing) adding the inspection characteristic. First doubt: why if operation of routing validity is 20.11.24, the key date of characteristic is 21.11.24?

This key-date (21.11.24) is then viewable in the inspection lot 03 as key-date, instead usually the key date in inspection lot corresponds to date of release of production order. Why this difference? 

Besides in this case the production order has been release in 28.11.24, so both 20.11 and 21.11 should go well for the system to read inspection characteristic and creating then the inspection point.

Where can be the problem? Someone has some suggests?

Thank you in advance,

Lorenzo

entry.title: Re: BAPI for transaction FB41 posting
entry.content: You can use a validation in OB28. Set up a validation to check if the tax code is missing for specific G/L accounts. If the tax code is empty, assign a default dummy tax code (e.g., XX) to bypass the error. This ensures compliance with account requirements while maintaining posting consistency.

entry.title: SAP Personas script loop Mass upade Equipment classification
entry.content:
Hi. I have a little bit tricky scenario.

I would like to mass update classification on equipment in transaction IE05 with this steps:

Multiple selection of equipment to be changed. Go to details.

It shows the first item of selected equipment. I have a classification on the left, which belongs to this equipment. No on the right I have custom fields, so I can select which classification I want to copy to other selected items.

Now, I have a button, that simulates "Save" of the equipment. By SAP standard it automatically goes to next selected item/equipment.

//Variable to be copied to all selected equipments
var vl = session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subPersonas_173150828914583/txtPersonas_173158981168825").text;
//button save
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").press()

//now I want to check if the equipment is actually there (so it stops at the end of selection)
if (session.idExists("wnd[0]/usr/subSUB_EQKO:SAPLITO0:0152/subSUB_0152A:SAPLITO0:1520/ctxtITOBATTR-EQUNR"))
{
    //just selecting the right tab
    session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTABSTRIP/tabpT\\03").select();
//now I want to enter the variable into selected equipment field
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTABSTRIP/tabpT\\03/ssubSUB_DATA:SAPLITO0:0109/subSUB_0109A:SAPLITO0:1093/subSUB_1090A:SAPLCTMS:4000/sub:SAPLCTMS:4000/ctxtRCTMS-MWERT[3,32]").text = vl;

//then I also enter variable in the same custom field, so it could read with next loop
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subPersonas_173150828914583/txtPersonas_173158981168825").text = vl;

//by now, I expet, that original variable vas transfered to next equipment and also put it self again for next equipment
//now I press the same script button, that originally started the script
    session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btnPersonas_17320111464853").press()}

The script successfully updates the second selected item, but not the rest

 

 

How could I do this loop, so it would go to all selected equipment and update the desired field?

entry.title: SAP Academic Community Conference Ibero-America 2024: What an Experience!
entry.content:
The SAP Academic Community Conference Ibero-America 2024  was an event to remember!

As you know, a key mission of SAP University Alliances is to build bridges between SAP, educational institutions, and the SAP ecosystem, and your engagement brings this vision to life! We were thrilled to have you with us for both our virtual sessions and at each of the six on-premises gatherings across SAP offices in Latin America.

Already feeling nostalgic for those #SAPACC moments? We’ve put together a quick recap to relive some of the highlights and celebrate the insights, discussions, and connections that made this event memorable...

 

#SAPACC recap – November 4th

Virtual Webinar

Our #SAPACC week kicked off with an inspiring webinar introducing SAP Next-Gen, showcasing our offerings to the academic community, and featuring several special sessions with SAP leaders on various key segments.

With over 190 participants, 8 hours of content, and 7 sessions, the event provided a unique opportunity to explore SAP’s of and the diverse opportunities for academic collaboration.

Participants explored key topics such as Cloud, Artificial Intelligence (AI), Sustainability, and Diversity & Inclusion (D&I), while also connecting with other members of our community. This session highlighted the valuable resources within SAP University Alliances that support your journey both in and beyond the classroom!

Looking for a recap of any sessions? You can access recordings and slides here: 

  • Opening: Welcome to the SAP Next-Gen World with Dr. Karina Edmonds  
  • SAP University Alliances with Maria Calzada & Gloria Sandi 
  • Academic Board Ibero-America with Jesus Aguillar 
  • Cloud at SAP with Alexandre Pereira & special guest
  • Sustainability at SAP with Renata Amorim 
  • AI at SAP with Matheus Souza 
  • Diversity and Inclusion at SAP with Ezequiel Massa 
  • Slides

 

#SAPACC recap – November 8th

Bogotá, Colombia

Our Bogotá event brought together 25 participants, who engaged in seven insightful sessions led by SAP experts and special panelists from Universidad de Los Andes and ERP4students.

Buenos Aires, Argentina

In Buenos Aires, 13 attendees participated in eight sessions. Contributions came from SAP experts, alongside panel discussions featuring leading institutions such as UNTREF, UADE, ERPsimLab, and a special appearance by Tecnológico de Monterrey.

Lima, Peru

Our event in Lima welcomed 24 participants who took part in four engaging sessions led by SAP experts and complemented by panels with insights from special guests from Universidad Peruana de Ciencias Aplicadas and Universidad de Lima.

Monterrey, Mexico

The Monterrey event brought together 24 attendees with an agenda featuring seven sessions presented by SAP experts and special panelists from Tecnológico de Monterrey offering valuable insights.

Santiago, Chile

In Santiago, 20 participants joined us for five sessions, featuring perspectives from our guest panelists from Universidad de las Américas and Universidad Bernardo O'Higgins, along with other special sessions led by SAP experts.

São Leopoldo, Brazil

Our largest event, in São Leopoldo, gathered 66 participants for ten inspiring sessions with SAP experts and contributions from professors from leading institutions such as FESP, FSA, Mackenzie, UCS, and UFRGS.

 

Thank you once again to everyone who attended our events, especially those who submitted panels and contributed their incredible insights and knowledge to the SAP Academic Community. It was a true pleasure to engage with each of you and learn about the amazing work being done, as well as the exciting plans for the future!

Share your favorite moments or key takeaways from the event in the comments below. We look forward to continuing to work together and strengthen our collaboration moving forward 💙
